capability_objective: Resolve Alder & Finch Books in West Region and Sunrise Food Pantry in Central Region with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Sunrise Food Pantry is still prospect, change only Sunrise Food Pantry from prospect to qualified. Leave Alder & Finch Books unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify account team with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two account records in the supplied CRM sandbox by using Pi's genuine Bash tool to execute the local `./crmctl` program. Begin by reading the program's built-in help, and use that executable as the sole interface for every CRM read, mutation, and notification. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or runtime data, its operation history, or anything under `.harness`, and do not modify those files or manufacture operation evidence.

Create an executable Bash program named `reconcile_accounts.sh`, then run it exactly once. In that program, search for the exact account name `Alder & Finch Books` in `West Region` and the exact account name `Sunrise Food Pantry` in `Central Region`. The two scoped searches are independent: start them concurrently as separate `crmctl` processes, then wait for both. Do not combine them into one broad search or run them sequentially. Require exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID from each result. If either search is missing or ambiguous, identify that lookup and stop before every retrieval, account mutation, or notification.

Only after both searches have finished and resolved uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by the IDs returned from their corresponding searches. Start the two retrievals concurrently as separate `crmctl` processes, then wait for both. Do not use an ID that was not returned by its corresponding search, and do not take status from a search result. If either complete record is missing, mismatches its search, or lacks a status, stop without changing account state or notifying anyone.

After both retrievals have completed, evaluate only the freshly retrieved status of `Sunrise Food Pantry`. If and only if that status is exactly `prospect`, change only its returned record to `qualified` with one later CRM operation. Leave `Alder & Finch Books` and every same-name, similarly named, related, and out-of-region record untouched. If the change is skipped or its command fails, do not send a notification. Only after the change command reports success, use the success receipt it returns to notify `account team` of that outcome with one later CRM operation. Do not create, delete, merge, cancel, or otherwise update an account; do not notify any other audience or perform any additional CRM write or notification. Temporary files made by the program must be removed on exit.

Have the program write `reconciliation.md` only after the applicable CRM path succeeds. That file must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullets with no heading, blank line, or extra prose. List the named records alphabetically in the first two bullets, each shaped `- <name> | <region> | <retrieved status>`. When the change and notification succeed, the third bullet must be exactly `- Action | changed Sunrise Food Pantry from prospect to qualified; notified account team after the change succeeded`. When the change is skipped, it must be exactly `- Action | left Sunrise Food Pantry unchanged because its retrieved status was <retrieved status>; no notification sent`, substituting the retrieved status. After the program finishes successfully, run `python3 .harness/verify.py`. Return the same three bullets in your final response.
